message:
Propagate serialization errors from audit hash computation instead of silently defaulting

compute_hash() previously used unwrap_or_default() on serde_json::to_string(),
which would silently produce an empty-string hash if serialization ever failed.
This masked potential data integrity issues in the audit chain.

Changes:
- AuditEntry::compute_hash() now returns Result<(), AuditError> with a
  Serialization variant, allowing callers (AuditLog::append) to surface
  failures through the existing error channel
- AuditEntry::verify_hash() returns false on serialization failure rather
  than comparing against a hash of an empty string
- compute_entry_hash() in verify.rs returns a sentinel "serialization_failed"
  string on error instead of silently hashing empty input
- Update test callsites to unwrap the new Result

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
